meta.note: Generated by Claude from world knowledge; coordinates approximate; dates best-known. Curated 2026-07-05.

items.name: Cortes Island
items.description: Island in the northern Salish Sea between Quadra Island and Desolation Sound; population ~1,000.

items.name: Whaletown
items.description: Hamlet on the west coast; ferry terminal, post office, library. Named for a short-lived whaling station.

items.name: Manson's Landing
items.description: Historic settlement and lagoon on the southwest coast; commercial and cultural hub with dock and provincial park.

items.name: Squirrel Cove
items.description: Sheltered anchorage and settlement on the east coast; general store, government dock, popular with Desolation Sound boaters.

items.name: T'oq (Klahoose Village)
items.description: Klahoose First Nation village at Squirrel Cove; administrative centre of the Nation on Cortes.

items.name: Seaford
items.description: Former settlement on the east side of the island facing Lewis Channel; now a rural residential area.

items.name: Gorge Harbour
items.description: Nearly landlocked harbour entered through a narrow cliff-walled gorge; marina and aquaculture.

items.name: Cortes Bay
items.description: East-side bay with government dock and outstations of Seattle and Royal Vancouver yacht clubs.

items.name: Smelt Bay Provincial Park
items.description: Beachfront park at the south end; camping, sunset views over Sutil Channel, historic smelt runs.

items.name: Manson's Landing Provincial Park
items.description: Day-use park spanning ocean lagoon, sand spit, and Hague Lake shoreline.

items.name: Háthayim (Von Donop) Marine Provincial Park
items.description: Large marine park protecting Von Donop Inlet on north Cortes; co-managed with Klahoose First Nation.

items.name: Kw'as Regional Park
items.description: Forest trail network between Hague and Gunflint lakes; second-growth and remnant old-growth fir.

items.name: Carrington Bay Regional Park
items.description: Remote park on the northwest coast with lagoon and rough trail access.

items.name: Hank's Beach Forest Conservation Park
items.description: Community-conserved forest and beach on the southeast coast near Cortes Bay.

items.name: Whaletown Commons
items.description: Community-acquired forest parcel in the heart of Whaletown, protected after a long fundraising campaign.

items.name: Children's Forest
items.description: Community-protected forest near Carrington Lagoon dedicated to nature education for island children.

items.name: Mitlenatch Island Nature Provincial Park
items.description: Seabird sanctuary island southwest of Cortes; largest seabird colony in the Strait of Georgia.

items.name: Hague Lake
items.description: Clear freshwater lake with sandy swimming beaches; drinking watershed for Manson's Landing area.

items.name: Gunflint Lake
items.description: Lake adjoining Hague Lake, ringed by Kw'as Park trails; warm summer swimming.

items.name: Robertson Lake
items.description: Small lake in the island's less-developed north; trail access.

items.name: Wiley Lake
items.description: Remote north-island lake near the Von Donop trail system.

items.name: Blue Jay Lake
items.description: Small lake and adjacent organic farm on the plateau above Gorge Harbour.

items.name: Manson's Lagoon
items.description: Tidal lagoon behind the spit at Manson's Landing; clam beds and bird habitat.

items.name: Von Donop Lagoon
items.description: Long tidal lagoon at the head of Von Donop Inlet; wilderness anchorage.

items.name: Squirrel Cove Lagoon
items.description: Tidal lagoon behind Squirrel Cove with reversing rapids at the entrance; kayak playground.

items.name: Sutil Point
items.description: Southern tip of Cortes with boulder reef extending offshore; navigational hazard and seal haul-out.

items.name: Reef Point
items.description: Point on the southwest shore near Smelt Bay; residential and farm area.

items.name: Mary Point
items.description: Southeast point facing Twin Islands and Desolation Sound; site of Klahoose-led development plans.

items.name: Bullock Bluff
items.description: Northern tip of Cortes Island where Lewis and Sutil channels meet; steep forested shoreline.

items.name: Quartz Bay
items.description: Northwest-coast bay with off-grid homesteads and shellfish tenures.

items.name: Coulter Bay
items.description: Bay north of Whaletown; quiet anchorage and rural neighbourhood.

items.name: Uganda Passage
items.description: Narrow dredged channel between Cortes and Marina Island's Shark Spit; dogleg navigation between markers.

items.name: Shark Spit
items.description: Long sand spit on Marina Island opposite Whaletown; picnic and shallow anchorage spot.

items.name: Marina Island
items.description: Undeveloped island southwest of Cortes, named by Spanish explorers for Cortés's interpreter La Malinche (Marina).

items.name: Twin Islands
items.description: Pair of private islands off southeast Cortes; historic lodge once visited by European royalty.

items.name: Sutil Channel
items.description: Channel separating Cortes from Quadra and Read islands; named for the Spanish schooner Sutil.

items.name: Lewis Channel
items.description: Channel between Cortes and West Redonda Island; main boating route to Desolation Sound.

items.name: Desolation Sound
items.description: Renowned cruising ground east of Cortes; named by George Vancouver in 1792.

items.name: Refuge Cove
items.description: Supply stop and store on West Redonda Island; the de facto marine hub for the Cortes area.

items.name: Teakerne Arm
items.description: Fjord on West Redonda with waterfall from Cassel Lake; day-trip destination from Cortes.

items.name: Green Mountain
items.description: Highest point on Cortes (~270 m); viewpoint over the Salish Sea and Coast Mountains.

items.name: Easter Bluff
items.description: Rocky bluff viewpoint on the Gorge Harbour uplands; short trail with views to Marina Island.

items.name: Hollyhock
items.description: Educational retreat centre on the southeast shore; workshops in wellness, leadership, and social change.

items.name: Cold Mountain Institute
items.description: Human-potential-movement retreat that preceded Hollyhock on the same site.

items.name: Linnaea Farm
items.description: Community land trust farm on Gunflint Lake; organic agriculture and ecological education since the 1980s.

items.name: Channel Rock
items.description: Off-grid waterfront homestead of writer Gilean Douglas, later an Ecotrust Canada learning centre.

items.name: Gilean Douglas
items.description: Writer, poet, and long-serving regional director who homesteaded at Channel Rock; author of 'The Protected Place'.

items.name: Manson Trading Post
items.description: Trading post established by Shetland-born Michael Manson, the island's first permanent settler family enterprise.

items.name: Whaletown Whaling Station
items.description: Dawson & Douglass whaling station that operated briefly and gave Whaletown its name.

items.name: Spanish Naming of Cortes
items.description: Galiano and Valdés chart the island and name it for conquistador Hernán Cortés.

items.name: Vancouver Expedition Survey
items.description: George Vancouver's expedition surveys adjacent waters, naming Desolation Sound during a joint season with the Spanish.

items.name: Klahoose First Nation
items.description: ɁayɁaǰuθəm-speaking Nation whose territory includes Cortes Island and Toba Inlet; main reserve at Squirrel Cove.

items.name: Clam Gardens
items.description: Ancient Klahoose mariculture terraces on Cortes beaches; rock-walled intertidal gardens boosting clam production.

items.name: Manson's Landing Middens
items.description: Deep shell middens marking millennia of Indigenous occupation around the lagoon and spit.

items.name: Union Steamship Era
items.description: Coastal steamships linked Cortes settlements to Vancouver, carrying freight, mail, and passengers.

items.name: Whaletown Post Office
items.description: One of the oldest continuously operating institutions on the island.

items.name: BC Ferries Route 24
items.description: Heriot Bay (Quadra) to Whaletown ferry service begins, ending steamer-era isolation.

items.name: MV Tachek
items.description: Long-serving small ferry on the Quadra–Cortes run.

items.name: Cortes Connection
items.description: Passenger van/freight service linking Cortes to Campbell River via two ferries.

items.name: Cortes Island Museum & Archives
items.description: Museum in a heritage store building at Manson's Landing; natural history, settler, and First Nations exhibits.

items.name: Wild Cortes
items.description: Natural history education centre at Linnaea Farm run with the Cortes Museum; specimen collections and ecology displays.

items.name: Mansons Hall
items.description: Community hall hosting the Friday Market, events, post office, and services; social centre of south Cortes.

items.name: Gorge Hall
items.description: Community hall above Gorge Harbour; dances, weddings, and gatherings for the west side.

items.name: St. John the Baptist Church
items.description: Small Anglican church serving Whaletown; heritage wooden building.

items.name: Cortes Island School
items.description: K-12 public school (SD72) at Manson's Landing.

items.name: Cortes Island Academy
items.description: Semester-based arts-and-ecology high school program drawing students from off-island.

items.name: Folk U
items.description: Community 'folk university' offering skill-sharing workshops and a radio hour.

items.name: CKTZ 89.5 FM
items.description: Cortes community radio station broadcasting island news, music, and public affairs.

items.name: Cortes Currents
items.description: Local news outlet (formerly the Tideline ecosystem) covering island governance, ecology, and culture.

items.name: Cortes Natural Food Co-op
items.description: Member-owned grocery at Manson's Landing; organic food hub and social nucleus.

items.name: Squirrel Cove General Store
items.description: Store, fuel, and takeout serving east-side residents and Desolation Sound boat traffic.

items.name: Gorge Harbour Marina Resort
items.description: Full-service marina with moorage, campground, pool, and store inside Gorge Harbour.

items.name: Floathouse Restaurant
items.description: Waterfront restaurant at Gorge Harbour Marina; seasonal hub for boaters and locals.

items.name: Friday Market
items.description: Weekly summer market at Mansons Hall; produce, crafts, prepared food, and live music.

items.name: Cortes Day
items.description: Annual community celebration at Smelt Bay with games, food, and music.

items.name: Shellfish Aquaculture
items.description: Oyster and clam farming, concentrated in Gorge Harbour and along east-coast tenures; a core island industry.

items.name: Klahoose Wilderness Resort
items.description: Klahoose-owned ecotourism lodge in Homfray Channel; grizzly tours in Toba Inlet, accessed via Cortes.

items.name: Klahoose Forestry Operations
items.description: Klahoose-led forestry and resource ventures managing tenure on and around Cortes.

items.name: Friends of Cortes Island
items.description: Environmental stewardship society running conservation, education, and monitoring programs.

items.name: Rainbow Ridge Housing Project
items.description: Cortes Community Housing Society's affordable-housing development near Manson's Landing.

items.name: Strathcona Regional District Area B
items.description: Cortes Island's local-government unit, represented by a single elected director.

items.name: Volunteer Fire Departments
items.description: Cortes fire protection delivered by volunteer halls at Manson's and Whaletown.

items.name: Cortes Health Centre
items.description: Community clinic providing primary care; physician and nursing services for the island.

items.name: Dillon Creek Wetland Restoration
items.description: Constructed wetland at Linnaea Farm restoring salmon-bearing creek habitat and filtering lake inputs.

items.name: Hague Lake Beach
items.description: Sandy public swimming beach; granite shelves and clear water, a summer institution.

items.name: Siskin Forest Trails
items.description: Community trail network through forest between Manson's Landing and Smelt Bay.

items.name: Old-Growth Douglas-fir Stands
items.description: Remnant old-growth veterans in Kw'as Park and the Children's Forest, survivors of early hand-logging.

items.name: Coastal Wolves
items.description: Sea wolves periodically swim to Cortes and den on the island; subject of local coexistence protocols.

items.name: Humpback Whale Return
items.description: Humpbacks recolonize the waters around Cortes after a century of absence; regular sightings in Sutil Channel.

items.name: Gorge Harbour Bioluminescence
items.description: Summer dinoflagellate blooms make night paddling in the Gorge glow; local kayak tour staple.

items.name: Rex Weyler
items.description: Greenpeace co-founder, author, and ecologist; long-time Cortes resident and public voice on island ecology.

items.name: Ruth Ozeki
items.description: Novelist and Zen priest with a home on Cortes; 'A Tale for the Time Being' draws on a Cortes-like island setting.

items.name: Hollyhock Garden
items.description: Kitchen and flower garden at Hollyhock; a horticultural showpiece feeding the retreat's kitchen.

items.name: Hand-Logging Era
items.description: Early 20th-century logging shaped nearly all Cortes forest; A-frame and steam-donkey sites remain as artifacts.

items.name: Whaletown Cemetery
items.description: Small pioneer cemetery serving the island's west side.

items.name: Salish Sea
items.description: Inland sea encompassing Cortes; the name was formally adopted on both sides of the border in 2009-2010.

items.name: Shared Traditional Territory
items.description: Cortes lies within the territories of the Klahoose, Tla'amin, and Homalco peoples, speakers of ɁayɁaǰuθəm.

items.name: 2021 Census Population
items.description: Cortes Island records a population of roughly 1,035, continuing slow growth and seasonal doubling in summer.

items.name: Social Change Institute
items.description: Hollyhock's flagship convening for nonprofit and movement leaders, held on-site for many years.

items.name: Whaletown Library
items.description: Tiny volunteer-run library beside the church green; among the smallest library buildings in BC.
